name: Arrangeable Intent
summary: The 2-D grid-placement layout model — independent widgets on one shared N-column plane, each with an {x,y,w,h} cell, dragged and resized with collision/compaction (Fork 2 + Fork 3a of #1384, the draggable-dashboard model). REUSES the shipped reorderable-list substrate (Pointer Events drag, roving-tabindex keyboard path, live-region announcement, atomic moveBefore) rather than overloading the `reorder` intent's 1-D order contract — 2-D placement is a distinct state shape (a flat `Array<{x,y,w,h}>`), not a sequence. Grid-snap is a dimension here (not a separate entity); magnetic peer alignment is the separate alignment-guides intent. Carries the APG Grid a11y contract.

compaction.description: How widgets settle when one is dropped or resized into another's cell — the collision policy. `vertical` floats widgets up to fill gaps (react-grid-layout default); `horizontal` compacts leftward; `none` leaves widgets where dropped, displacing collided neighbors; `allow-overlap` permits widgets to stack on the same cells (free placement). Most-permissive default: `vertical`.

snap.description: Whether positions quantize to the grid — `grid` snaps {x,y,w,h} to whole column/row cells (the dashboard default, structurally part of THIS model — grid-snap is a dimension, not a standalone concern); `free` allows sub-cell pixel placement (a free canvas). Peer-relative magnetic alignment is a different concern entirely — the standalone alignment-guides intent.

resize.description: Whether widgets can be resized in place (grow/shrink their w/h via an edge/corner handle) in addition to being moved. `enabled` (default) exposes resize handles; `disabled` makes widgets move-only (fixed footprint).

description:
What This Is

The flat 2-D grid layout model (Fork 2 + Fork 3a of #1384): independent widgets on one shared N-column plane, each holding an {x, y, w, h} cell, freely dragged and resized with collision/compaction — the draggable-dashboard model (react-grid-layout, gridstack). Its state is a clean flat Array<{i,x,y,w,h}>, structurally distinct from a 1-D sequence (reorder) and from a recursive partition tree (dock/tile, deferred #1437).

Reuses the reorder substrate, doesn't overload it

2-D placement composes the shipped Reorderable List substrate — the Pointer Events drag, the roving-tabindex keyboard path, the live-region announcement, and the atomic Element.moveBefore() relocation (preserves focus/animation/connection) — rather than bending reorder's 1-D order contract into a 2-D coordinate model. Same mechanics, different state shape: a new intent, not a new dimension on reorder.

Dimensions

  • compaction: vertical (float up — default) / horizontal / none (displace) / allow-overlap (stack).
  • snap: grid (quantize to cells — default; grid-snap is a dimension of this model) vs free (sub-cell pixel placement).
  • resize: enabled (resize handles — default) vs disabled (move-only).

Fixed a11y invariant — APG Grid

Keyboard parity is part of the contract: the surface follows the WAI-ARIA APG Grid pattern (arrow navigation between widgets, a grab/move/drop key model with live-region announcement of each move and the final placement), inherited from the reorder substrate. A pointer-only arrangeable is non-conforming.

The snap seam

Per the spatial survey, "snap" is two things: grid-snap is this intent's snap dimension; magnetic peer alignment (Figma smart guides) is the separate alignment-guides intent that composes arrangeable. scroll-snap (carousel paging) is a third, unrelated platform feature. Grounded in the spatial-manipulation survey (spatial-manipulation arrangeable surfaces).
